title: Modern Platform Bed Frame (All Sizes)
summary: A minimal 2x6 box-frame platform bed on hairpin legs, with 2x4 center supports and 1x3 slats — in five genuinely distinct sizes from twin through California king, not one size scaled by feel.
description:
Every size shares the same design: a 2x6 box frame (side rails plus a top-and-bottom end piece), 2x4 center supports running the length for slat backup, and 1x3 slats spanning the width, all standing on hairpin legs rather than a separate leg-and-apron system — this is what gives the bed its minimal, modern silhouette. **Larger sizes add more center supports** (one for twin, two for full/queen, three for king/cal king) since a wider bed needs more mid-span backup to keep the slats from sagging.

Optional decorative end pieces, beveled at 45° on both ends, dress up the head and foot of the frame — these are explicitly optional and can be skipped for an even plainer look. Center legs get their own short cut length depending on whether 6" or 8" hairpin legs are used, since the two heights aren't interchangeable without adjusting this piece.

**Each size below is a complete, distinct cut list** — twin (38"x75"), full (54"x75"), queen (60"x80"), standard king (76"x80"), and California king (72"x84") — not one frame scaled by a formula. Build only the size needed; the other four sections are reference for anyone building a different size later.

materials.name: Hairpin legs, 6 in or 8 in, with mounting screws
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Ends cut at 45° bevel, ends not parallel, long point to long point.
Ends cut at 45° bevel, ends not parallel, long point to long point.
Cut at 11-1/2" instead if using 8" hairpin legs.
Ends cut at 45° bevel, ends not parallel, long point to long point.

steps.title: Choose your size and cut the frame
steps.body: Select the size you're building and cut the side rails, top and bottom, and center supports for that size only.

steps.title: Build the box frame
steps.body: Assemble the side rails with the top and bottom pieces into a square box, then add the center supports running lengthwise.

steps.title: Add the optional decorative ends
steps.body: Miter and attach the decorative end pieces if wanted for a finished look.

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Attach the center legs (cut to match your hairpin leg height) and mount the hairpin legs at all attachment points.
materials: Hairpin legs, 6 in or 8 in, with mounting screws

steps.title: Add the slats
steps.body: Screw the slats down across the frame and center supports for your chosen size.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly
